children.html: β^=(XTX)−1XTY=(XTX)−1XT(Xβ+u)=(XTX)−1XTX⏟=Iβ+(XTX)−1XTu=β+(XTX)−1XTu=β+(1nXTX)−11nXTu\begin{aligned} \hat{\beta} &= (X^T X)^{-1} X^T Y\\ &= (X^T X)^{-1} X^T (X\beta + u)\\ &= \underbrace{ (X^T X)^{-1} X^T X}_{=I} \beta + (X^T X)^{-1} X^T u\\ &= \beta + (X^T X)^{-1} X^T u\\ &= \beta + \left(\frac{1}{n} X^T X \right)^{-1} \frac{1}{n} X^T u\\ \end{aligned}β^​​=(XTX)−1XTY=(XTX)−1XT(Xβ+u)==I(XTX)−1XTX​​β+(XTX)−1XTu=β+(XTX)−1XTu=β+(n1​XTX)−1n1​XTu​

children.value: パッケージのドキュメント
鹿野繁樹. (2015). 新しい計量経済学: データで因果関係に迫る.
不均一分散 — Pythonで学ぶ入門計量経済学

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

n = 100
np.random.seed(0)
x1 = np.random.uniform(0, 5, size=(n, 1))
x0 = np.ones(shape=(n, 1))
X = np.append(x0, x1, axis=1)
beta = np.array([3, 5])

# 均一分散の場合
e = np.random.normal(loc=0, scale=1, size=n)
y = X @ beta + e

# plot
fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.scatter(x1, y)

beta_hat = np.linalg.inv(X.T @ X) @ X.T @ y
beta_hat
